title: Event Automation Rules
description: VS Code extension rules. Each rule watches for VS Code events and publishes them via UDP to a desktop utility.

publishTo.description: UDP target(s) for publish actions. e.g. "udp://127.0.0.1:38471"

commands.description: Custom palette command IDs that emit command.<id> events.

on.description: Event name(s) that trigger this rule, e.g. "test.passed" or ["task.finished", "test.failed"]

cooldown.description: Minimum time between firings, e.g. "5s" or "2m"

once.description: Fire at most once per session.

priority.description: Higher runs first when multiple rules match.

stop.description: Stop evaluating further rules after this one fires.

execute.description: Whether do[] actions run one-by-one (default) or all at once.

action.description: One action to perform when the rule fires.

publish.description: Event type to publish via UDP, e.g. "test.passed"

to.description: Override publish target(s) for this action only.

payload.description: Key-value pairs forwarded in the event. String values support ${variable} interpolation.

parallel.description: Run a group of actions concurrently; continues to next step only when all finish.

notify.description: Show a VS Code notification with this message.

command.description: VS Code command to run when clicked.

run.description: Shell command to run when clicked.
Run a shell command in the integrated terminal.

cwd.description: Working directory for the run action.

command.description: Execute a VS Code command by ID.

args.description: Arguments passed to the VS Code command.

webhook.description: Send an HTTP request to this URL.

statusBar.description: Show text in the VS Code status bar.

timeout.description: How long to show the status bar item, e.g. "4s".
